# Scuffers Drop Co-Pilot

> Operational AI Control Tower for high-demand streetwear drops.
> Hackathon UDIA × Scuffers × ESIC 2026.

## What this is

A real-time decision system that:
- Loads 6 CSVs (orders, customers, inventory, support tickets, campaigns, order_items)
- Calls the live Shipping Status API (`https://lkuutmnykcnbfmbpopcu.functions.supabase.co/api/shipping-status/{order_id}`) for ~50 relevant orders
- Generates a Top 10 of prioritized actions (max 10 per challenge spec) with: title, reason, expected impact, confidence, owner, € at risk, € recoverable, VIPs affected, pre-built customer message
- Computes a Counterfactual Twin (without plan vs with plan, in €)
- Produces a single Drop Health Score 0-100 (executive KPI)
- Allows real-time execution of actions via mobile, with state mutation, multi-channel notification (Telegram, Discord, Slack), and live dashboard updates

## How to verify (for evaluators)

| Endpoint | Description |
|---|---|
| `GET /` | Mobile-first dashboard (login dummy: any name + role) |
| `GET /api/data` | Full state JSON: actions + simulation + health |
| `GET /api/evaluate` | **Auto-evaluation report**: criteria coverage, self-checks (22 assertions) |
| `GET /api/shipping` | Live Shipping API integration status & issues |
| `GET /api/state` | Current execution state |
| `POST /api/agent` | Conversational AI assistant (NL → action) |
| `POST /api/run` | Re-run the engine end-to-end |
| `GET /webhook/execute-action?type=X&id=Y` | Execute an action (mutates state, notifies channels) |
| `POST /webhook/drop-copilot-run` | Fan-out trigger for Telegram + Discord pinned panels |

## Architecture

```
6 CSVs → control_tower.py (engine)
            ├─ lib/loader.py       (robust CSV loading)
            ├─ lib/shipping_api.py (live external API w/ cache + error handling)
            ├─ lib/candidates.py   (8 detectors / 5 lenses · ~67 candidates)
            ├─ ranking             (top 10 with owner balancing)
            ├─ lib/simulator.py    (counterfactual twin)
            ├─ lib/ai.py           (Gemini 2.5-flash + deterministic fallback)
            └─ lib/humanize.py     (translation: SKU codes → natural language)
            └─→ out/actions.json + simulation.json + health_score.json + EVALUATION_REPORT.md
                    ↓
           webapp.py (Flask, single-file)
            ├─ Mobile-first dashboard (Scuffers branding)
            ├─ Real-time clock & auto-refresh
            ├─ Sticky subnav, filters by owner/VIP, dummy login w/ 5 roles
            ├─ Live shipping feed (auto-refresh 5s)
            ├─ Conversational AI chat widget (POST /api/agent)
            └─ Webhook endpoints for action execution
                    ↓
        ┌──────────┴───────────┐
        ↓                      ↓
  TELEGRAM (act)         DISCORD (view)
  conversational AI      pinned panel + 10 cards
  + telegram_agent.py    + logs channel (separate)
```

## Channel division

- **Discord = view-only** dashboard (pinned panel that auto-edits + 10 actions cards)
- **Telegram = act** via natural language ("ejecuta la 1", "qué pasa con los envíos") — the bot is an AI assistant powered by Gemini

## Stack

- Python 3.9+ (Flask, urllib, json, no heavy deps)
- Gemini 2.5-flash (with deterministic fallback)
- ngrok (public URL for hackathon)
- Telegram Bot API (polling worker)
- Discord webhooks (regular + logs channel)

## Files of interest

- `arquitectura.md` — 3-line data flow + business rule
- `pitch_fundadores.md` — 60s pitch
- `COMO_FUNCIONA.md` — full technical guide for the team
- `PARA_LOS_JUECES.md` — guide for evaluators
- `EVALUATION_REPORT.md` — auto-generated criteria-vs-evidence map (after engine run)
- `control_tower.py` — main engine (~250 LOC)
- `webapp.py` — single-file web app (~1000 LOC HTML+CSS+JS+Python)
- `lib/agent_tools.py` — agent tool registry & dispatcher
- `telegram_agent.py` — Telegram polling worker

## Live URL (during hackathon)

https://walmart-outage-frolic.ngrok-free.dev

## Built with

Claude Code (entirely AI-assisted development).
